DARK: with little or no light; gloomy
DAYLIGHT: the light from the sun during day
DEARTH: a scarcity or lack of something
DECEPTIVE: giving an appearance different from true nature
DECISIVE: settling an issue; producing definite result
DECK: a floor of a ship; pack of cards
DECREASE: to become smaller or fewer in number
DEEM: to regard or consider in specified way
DEFEND: to resist attack; protect from harm
DEFICIENT: not having enough of specified quality
DEFICIT: the amount by which something is lacking
DEFILE: to damage purity or appearance of
DEFINE: to state meaning of word or nature of
DEFINITION: statement of exact meaning of word
DEFY: to openly resist or refuse to obey
DELUGE: a heavy fall of rain; flood
DEMONSTRATE: to clearly show existence of something
DENIAL: the action of declaring something untrue
DENY: to state that something is not true
DEPICT: to show or represent by drawing
DETER: to discourage someone from doing something
DEVELOP: to grow or cause to grow
DESIGN: to plan and make for specific purpose
DESPISE: to feel contempt or deep repugnance for
DESTROY: to put end to existence of
DIFFIDENCE: modesty or shyness from lack of confidence
DIFFIDENT: modest or shy because of lack confidence
DIM: not shining brightly; not clearly visible
DISCARD: to get rid of as no longer useful
DISCLOSURE: action of making new information known
DISGUISE: to conceal identity by changing appearance
DISGUST: feeling of revulsion or strong disapproval
DISMISS: to order to leave; reject as unworthy
DISPLACEMENT: action of moving something from position
DISPARAGE: to regard as being of little worth
DISSENT: to hold or express opinions at variance
DISTANCE: length of space between two points
DISTANT: far away in space or time
DISTILL: to extract essence by heating and cooling
DISTINGUISH: to perceive or point out difference
DISTORT: to pull or twist out of shape
DIVE: to plunge head first into water
DIVERGE: to separate and go in different direction
DOCK: area of water where ships are loaded
DOCUMENT: written or printed paper providing record
DODGE: to avoid by sudden quick movement
DONATE: to give money or goods for good cause
DRAFT: rough first version; current of air
DRASTICALLY: in way that has far-reaching effects
DRILL: to make hole with pointed tool; practice
DRIFT: to be carried slowly by current
DRIVE: to operate motor vehicle; to urge forward
DROP: to let fall; small amount of liquid
DROUGHT: prolonged period of abnormally low rainfall
DUE: owed as debt; expected at certain time
DWELL: to live in or at specified place
